definition: An organized way to store and relate data so operations can be performed effectively.
A behavioral interface defined by operations rather than implementation.
A contiguous indexed structure for fast position-based access.
A sequence useful for traversal and ordered grouping.
A last-in-first-out structure for nested reversal and backtracking.
A first-in-first-out structure for ordered service.
A structure associating keys with values.
A structure using hashes for fast membership or key-value lookup.
A parent-child structure for hierarchy recursion and parsing.
A tree-shaped structure supporting efficient priority retrieval.
A node-edge structure for relationships paths and networks.
A structure that speeds lookup and retrieval.
A coordinate representation for similarity magnitude and transformation.
A condition that must remain true for a structure to remain valid.
